title: Paper-based electrochemical biosensor for C-reactive protein detection in whole blood
abstract: A disposable paper-based electrochemical immunosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies on screen-printed gold electrodes detected C-reactive protein in undiluted whole blood with a limit of detection of 0.3 mg/L within 8 minutes, comparable to laboratory ELISA performance.

title: Lateral flow immunoassay for rapid CRP quantification at point of care
authors: Wang H, Zhang J, et al.
abstract: We report a colloidal-gold lateral flow strip with anti-CRP capture and detection antibodies enabling semi-quantitative CRP measurement in 5 minutes at the bedside without sample pretreatment.

title: Screen-printed electrode immunosensors for inflammation biomarkers
authors: Patel R, Hernandez D, et al.
abstract: Review of screen-printed electrode platforms for cytokine and acute-phase protein detection, including CRP, IL-6, and procalcitonin, with emphasis on clinical translation and whole-blood compatibility.

title: Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy for label-free CRP detection
abstract: Label-free EIS-based detection of CRP on gold microelectrodes functionalized via thiolated antibody fragments achieved sub-mg/L sensitivity in serum samples.

title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG protects intestinal barrier in DSS-induced colitis mice
abstract: Oral administration of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG to C57BL/6 mice for 28 days reduced FITC-dextran translocation by 35% and upregulated claudin-1 and occludin expression in colon tissue, ameliorating DSS colitis-associated barrier dysfunction.

title: Probiotic modulation of intestinal tight junction proteins: a systematic review
abstract: Review of probiotic effects on epithelial tight junctions. Multiple Lactobacillus species, including L. rhamnosus GG, increased ZO-1, claudin-1, and occludin expression and decreased paracellular permeability across in vitro and rodent models.

title: FITC-dextran assay for assessing intestinal permeability in mice: protocol
abstract: Step-by-step protocol for in vivo intestinal permeability assessment in mice using oral gavage of 4 kDa FITC-dextran followed by serum fluorescence quantification 4 hours later.

title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG and gut barrier integrity: mechanistic insights
abstract: Soluble factors from L. rhamnosus GG protect epithelial cells via PKC and MAP kinase signalling, leading to increased tight junction protein expression and reduced epithelial permeability.

title: Trehalose as a cryoprotectant for mammalian cell freezing
abstract: Intracellular trehalose loading via genetic expression or membrane-permeabilizing techniques improves post-thaw viability of mammalian cells, suggesting superior membrane stabilization compared to standard DMSO-only protocols.

title: Comparative cryopreservation of HeLa cells with DMSO, glycerol and sugars
abstract: Trehalose and sucrose supplementation in DMSO-based freezing media increased post-thaw HeLa cell viability by 10-20 percentage points relative to DMSO alone, attributed to improved membrane phase behaviour at low temperatures.

title: DMSO-free cryopreservation media for cell-based therapies
abstract: Sugar-based cryoprotectant cocktails containing trehalose, sucrose and hydroxyethyl starch maintain post-thaw viability above 80% for several mammalian cell lines while eliminating DMSO toxicity concerns.

title: Membrane stabilization mechanisms of trehalose at low temperatures
abstract: Biophysical review of trehalose's water-replacement and vitrification mechanisms, explaining its superior protection of phospholipid bilayers during freezing and desiccation.

title: Microbial electrosynthesis of acetate from CO2 by Sporomusa ovata
abstract: Sporomusa ovata biofilms on graphite cathodes poised at -400 mV vs SHE reduced CO2 to acetate with current efficiencies exceeding 85%, establishing the first microbial electrosynthesis platform for direct CO2 fixation.

title: Improving rates of microbial electrosynthesis with cathode materials
abstract: Carbon-felt and reticulated vitreous carbon cathodes increased acetate production rates by Sporomusa ovata to >120 mmol/L/day at -400 mV vs SHE, identifying surface area and biocompatibility as key drivers.

title: Bioelectrochemical CO2 reduction: state of the art and benchmarks
abstract: Comprehensive review of microbial electrosynthesis benchmarks, identifying acetate production rates of 100-150 mmol/L/day as the current ceiling for autotrophic acetogen-based systems.

title: Cathode potential effects on microbial electrosynthesis efficiency
abstract: Variation of cathode potential between -300 and -600 mV vs SHE in Sporomusa-based MES showed peak coulombic efficiency at -400 mV, with H2 evolution dominating at more negative potentials.

title: MIQE Guidelines: Minimum Information for Publication of Quantitative Real-Time PCR Experiments
abstract: Consensus guidelines specifying the minimum experimental detail required for transparent and reproducible qPCR experiments, covering RNA quality, primer validation, reference gene selection, and statistical analysis.

title: Standard protocol for HeLa cell culture and maintenance
abstract: Maintenance of HeLa (CCL-2) cells in EMEM with 10% FBS, subculturing at 80% confluence using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA, freezing in 90% complete medium + 10% DMSO at 1e6 cells/vial.

title: Quantitative ELISA for serum CRP: optimisation and validation
abstract: Sandwich ELISA protocol for human CRP using anti-CRP capture and biotinylated detection antibody pairs, with linear range 0.05-10 mg/L in serum and reproducibility CV<10%.

title: Tight junction architecture in the intestinal epithelium
abstract: Review of intestinal epithelial tight junction structure and regulation, including the roles of claudin-1, occludin, and ZO-1 in maintaining selective paracellular barrier function.

title: Antibody immobilisation strategies for biosensor electrodes
abstract: Comparison of self-assembled monolayer, protein A, and EDC/NHS coupling strategies for orienting antibodies on gold and carbon electrodes, with implications for biosensor sensitivity and stability.

title: Animal welfare considerations for C57BL/6 mouse studies
abstract: ARRIVE 2.0 guidelines for designing, conducting, and reporting in vivo studies in mice, with particular attention to sample size justification, randomisation, and blinding.

title: Sucrose vs trehalose in the cryopreservation of stem cells
abstract: Comparative study of sucrose- and trehalose-supplemented freezing media on stem cell post-thaw recovery; trehalose conferred a 12-18 percentage point advantage attributed to glass-transition stabilisation.

title: Bioelectrochemical reactor design for autotrophic acetogens
abstract: Reactor design considerations for microbial electrosynthesis with Sporomusa and Clostridium species, covering cathode materials, gas delivery, electrolyte composition and biofilm management.

title: Anti-CRP monoclonal antibody panel: epitope mapping and affinity
abstract: Characterisation of commercially available anti-human CRP monoclonal antibodies (clones C2, C5, C7) for epitope coverage and affinity, informing capture/detection pair selection in immunoassays.

title: Intestinal permeability assays: from in vitro Transwell to in vivo FITC-dextran
abstract: Methods review covering Caco-2 Transwell TEER, Ussing chambers, and in vivo FITC-dextran gavage assays with detailed protocols and interpretation guidance for gut barrier studies.

title: Cryopreservation viability assays: trypan blue, MTT, and clonogenic survival
abstract: Comparison of immediate and long-term post-thaw viability assays. Trypan blue exclusion overestimates survival; clonogenic assays remain the gold standard for proliferative recovery.

title: Sporomusa ovata cultivation in defined media for electrochemistry
abstract: Anaerobic cultivation protocol for Sporomusa ovata DSM 2662 in defined fructose-free medium under H2/CO2, with biomass quantification and inoculation conditions for bioelectrochemical reactors.

title: Acetate quantification by HPLC and enzymatic assays
abstract: Quantification methods for short-chain fatty acids including acetate from microbial cultures using ion-exclusion HPLC with refractive index detection, and enzymatic acetate kinase assays.

title: Western blot quantification of tight junction proteins claudin-1 and occludin
abstract: Standardised western blot protocol for quantification of claudin-1, occludin, and ZO-1 in intestinal tissue homogenates, with normalisation against beta-actin and densitometric analysis.

title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG ATCC 53103 culture and dosing
abstract: Cultivation of L. rhamnosus GG in MRS broth at 37 C anaerobically to stationary phase, with CFU enumeration and dosing recommendations for in vivo studies (typically 1e8-1e9 CFU/day).

title: Polymer Encapsulation of Sporomusa ovata for Stable Bioelectrosynthesis
abstract: Alginate encapsulation of Sporomusa ovata on graphite cathodes increased acetate productivity to 165 mmol/L/day, exceeding planktonic biofilm benchmarks by 25% under -400 mV vs SHE operation.

title: Statistical considerations for animal experiments using factorial designs
abstract: Practical guidance on sample size determination, randomisation, and analysis of variance for in vivo studies in rodents, with worked examples for treatment-vs-control comparisons.

title: Cyclic voltammetry of microbial cathode biofilms
abstract: Methodology paper on chronoamperometry and cyclic voltammetry of acetogen biofilms on cathodes, including reference electrode placement, scan-rate selection, and biofilm characterisation.

title: Bio-protocol: Quantitative western blot for membrane proteins
abstract: Step-by-step quantitative western blot protocol optimised for membrane-bound proteins like claudins and occludin, including extraction, sample prep, transfer, and densitometric quantification.

title: Disposable screen-printed electrodes for point-of-care diagnostics
abstract: Review of screen-printed electrode fabrication, modification chemistries, and applications in point-of-care immunoassays, with discussion of CRP and cardiac marker assay formats.

title: Open-source potentiostat designs for low-cost electrochemistry
abstract: Open-source potentiostat (CheapStat) for educational and prototype use, supporting cyclic voltammetry, square-wave voltammetry, and chronoamperometry up to ±1.5 V.

title: Experiment Plan Rubric — Chunk 2
abstract:
0.51
Related protocols
PR1. Bioelectrochemical reactor setup for microbial electrosynthesis with Sporomusa ovata (JOVE) [link]
Anaerobic two-chamber MES reactor build, cathode preparation, S. ovata inoculation, and acetate quantification.
PR2. HPLC quantification of short-chain fatty acids (acetate, propionate, butyrate) (Bio-protocol) [link]
Ion-exclusion HPLC with refractive index detection for SCFA quantification from microbial cultures and gut samples.

Protocol
Step 1. Prepare bioelectrochemical reactor (1 day)
Assemble an anaerobic two-chamber MES reactor as described in PR1. Ensure the cathode is set to a potential of
−400mV vs SHE.
References: PR1
Step 2. Prepare Sporomusa ovata culture (2 days)
Inoculate Sporomusa ovata in an appropriate growth medium and incubate under anaerobic conditions at 30°C until an
OD600 of 0.6 is reached.
References: P1
Step 3. Inoculate reactor with Sporomusa ovata (1 hour)
Transfer the Sporomusa ovata culture into the cathode chamber of the reactor. Ensure the chamber is sealed to maintain
anaerobic conditions.
References: PR1
Step 4. Operate reactor under experimental conditions (7 days)
Maintain the reactor at a cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE. Continuously supply CO■ at a flow rate of 10 mL/min.
Monitor the system for 7 days.
References: P1, PR1
Step 5. Quantify acetate production (2 hours per sample)
Collect samples from the cathode chamber daily. Use ion-exclusion HPLC with refractive index detection to quantify
acetate concentra
